settings.title: We Are Now Set Up for Hybrids and EVs
settings.excerpt: Two of our techs are freshly qualified on high-voltage systems, so your hybrid no longer means a trip to the dealer.
settings.body:
More of you arrive in hybrids and electrics every month, and until now we could only do the basics on them. That changes today.

Sam and Liam have completed their high-voltage qualifications, and we have invested in the insulated tools and equipment the work demands. We can now service hybrids and full EVs safely in-house, which means you no longer have to drive past us to reach a dealer.

What we can now do

The qualification is not a formality. Working around a high-voltage battery safely means knowing how to isolate the system, when to leave it alone, and which jobs need the special tooling. That training is exactly what used to send your car back to the main agent at main-agent prices.

  • Brakes, suspension, and tyres
  • Software-aware servicing to manufacturer schedules
  • High-voltage system checks and battery health

One thing worth knowing: hybrids and EVs are often kinder on some parts and harder on others. Regenerative braking means pads can last far longer than you expect, but the extra weight works the tyres and suspension harder. We will tell you what your car actually needs, not what a fixed schedule assumes.

If you have been told your hybrid can only be touched by a main agent, bring it to Torque and let us quote against it. We think you will be surprised at the difference, and we are happy to explain exactly what the work involves before you commit.
